sectionMetadata.label: Plan of Care and Physical Therapy Evaluation
Footer (Not Visible in Print Preview)
sectionMetadata.notes: Fax server headers, signatures, and page chrome should stay out of the printable review surface.

textSpans.text: Patient Address: 1054 Plentywood PL, Henderson IVV 89002
Patient words: 90-year-old male returns to the office with his daughter.
Patient underwent revascularization with vascular surgery.
daughter continues to do wound care at home.
The patient is a 90 year old male who presents for a follow-up visit.
Patient's blister has been drained however
I spoke to the vascular surgeon who performed the procedure stating the patient has
significant disease within the SFA and the patient is not candidate for bypass surgery.
The patient will require skilled wound care and nursing at home.
referral for a at-home nurse to perform dressing changes every other day was placed with his insurance.
Toe ulcer, left, with fat layer exposed (L97.522)
L03.116) Cellulitis of foot, left ( L03
He still has pain with significant swelling to the lower extremities.
Blood pressure not pertinent to visit
No varicosities noted, no pain to bilateral calves with
size, swelling, draiange, odor, redness, pain, fevers, chills.
Iron (ferrous sulfate) (325 mg(65 mg iro tablet, oral) Active.
Toprol XL (25mg Tablet, Extende, oral) Active.
Gastrointestinal Not Present- Diarrhea and Nausea.
palpation, capillary refill brisk x10, pedal hair was present bilaterally, no pitting edema noted bilaterally, normal skin
I recommended wound cleansing with Betadine gauze to the dorsum of the foot and iodoform to the medial aspect of the
- Cleanse ulcer daily with normal saline or antimicrobial cleanser during
Although having better inflow to the lower extremity he still significantly at risk for limb ischemia to his foot.
- Now status post revascularization however the limb is still at risk due to his significant peripheral arterial disease.
Musculoskeletal examination: Tenderness palpation to the left forefoot with edema
- Discussed the importance of strict glycemic control as it's related to healing diabetic foot ulcers

referralDashboardSections.label: Plan of Care and Physical Therapy Evaluation

textSpans.text: 5670 W Flamingo Rd, Ste D, Las Vegas NV 89103
Primary Needs for Home Health (check all that apply):
When We call, we will also need to obtain the following information before seeing the
If you have these available, feel free to fax along with this form.
Recent H&P or recent progress notes
clotrimazole ( topical) Specific strength unknown - Active.
No pertinent past surgical history Phreesia 03/17/2026
Current Rehab Status: No Current Drug Rehab 03/05/2024
Past Rehab Status: No Past Drug Rehab 03/05/2024
Surgical Blood Transfusion: Approves surgical blood transfusion 03/05/2024
Tobacco use: Current Tobacco User, Smokes 1 pack of cigarettes per day 03/05/2024
General Not Present- Chills and Fever.
Neurological Not Present- Loss of Consciousness.
General: AAOX3, well nourished, no acute distress
Vascular exam: No palpable pulses to the left foot.
On Doppler examination there was a monophasic signal to the both DP
and PT arteries, Dependent rubor noted to the distal left foot.
Neurologic examination: Epicritic sensation intact bilaterally, vibratory sensation was normal bilaterally
Dermatological examination: Toenails are significantly dystrophic, discolored with subungual debris X 101
Large serous blister noted to the dorsum of the left foot.
Left second toe on the medial aspect has a full-thickness ulceration down to adipose tissue.
Radiographs: General Left foot radiographs 3 views: No fractures or dislocations.
No gas-forming organisms in the left foot.
General Appearance in no acute distress, Healthy and Normal appearance.
Appropriate fund of knowledge, pupils equal and reactive to light and accommodation, normal sensation of trigeminal nerves,
Neurologic evaluation reveals - normal attention span and ability to concentrate, able to name objects and repeat phrases.
symmetrical functioning of hypoglossal nerve, normal sensation, normal coordination and upper and lower extremity deep
symmetrical functioning of facial nerves, normal hearing, normal gag reflex, symmetrical functioning of accessory nerves,
Joints and Muscles - Normal joints and muscles.
Note: 90-year-old male with a history of acute on chronic peripheral artery disease with left second toe ulcer.
previously treated for cellulitis last visit with clindamycin which has improved.
continues to have significant serous drainage from the dorsum of the foot due to his edema.
He does have reconstitution of three-
vessel runoff down to his foot however does have small vessel disease within the foot.
I have no plans performing emputation at this time.
Medihoney dry sterile dressings were performed to the left second toe.
I recommended the use of antibiotic ointment and
Band-Aid to the left toe at this time.
- Pt education on all local and systemic signs of infections Including changes in
- Reviewed the importance of healthy balanced diet and drinking water
- Advised to increase protein intake with Pro-Stat AWC or comprable supplement
- Improve glycemic control and increase protein

chartSnapshot.primary_reason_for_home_health_medical_n: James Helms, a 90-year-old male, is being referred for home health care following an encounter for change or removal of a non-surgical wound dressing, with a chronic ulcer on another part of the left foot with the fat layer exposed; he recently underwent a revascularization procedure due to significant Peripheral Artery Disease and has a left second toe ulceration at risk for infection requiring skilled wound care, with additional conditions including cellulitis of the left foot and a blister on the left foot, and
